instore.name: In-Store
instore.description: Set up for physical point-of-sale terminals

steps.title: Merchant and Store Setup
steps.description: Register your merchant and set up your store to start accepting payments.

sections.title: Create Merchant and Setup Store
sections.description: Register your merchant and set up your store to start accepting payments. You will get the merchant Id and store Id when you complete the webKYB setup. You can complete that through the webkyburl received in the following response.

sections.title: Get Application Status
sections.description: Check the status of your application.

steps.title: Terminal Registration
steps.description: Select your terminal based on your requirements.

sections.title: Register with SurfTester App
sections.description: Use the SurfTester app to register your terminal.

surfTester.description: Use SurfTester app to test your terminal integration

ios.supportText: Contact support to get an invite mail to download the app

surfTester.footnote: SurfTester is available for testing terminal integrations

sections.title: Register Terminal
sections.description: Connect and activate your payment terminal with your merchant account by registering the terminal.

steps.title: Implement Payment Flow
steps.description: Set up the core payment flow for your integration.

prerequisites.text: At least one product with ID, name, price, tax, and currency

implementationSteps.title: Create New Order
implementationSteps.description: You can create an order with adjustments such as tips or other changes that affect the total order amount or without it. In the following example tips is included as adjustment

metaData.key: This is the tip from order lines

prerequisites.label: Order created and returned an orderId
Payment terminal registered and activated (for in-store payments)

implementationSteps.title: Initiate Payment
implementationSteps.description: Initiate payment for order you have created in the previous step via Initiate Payment API. This typically happens when the customer taps their card during in-store payments.

implementationSteps.title: Fetch Order Status
implementationSteps.description: Once you initiate the payment, you check it's status via Fetch Order Status API.

steps.title: Update Order

prerequisites.text: Order created and in a modifiable state (e.g., not yet paid or captured)

steps.description: Let’s say you created an order with two line items Orange and Grape juice (totaling 210 SEK). Now you want to add one more item, Bread (100 SEK), to the same order using the same orderId before initiating the payment.

sections.title: Create Order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created with 2 line items

sections.title: Update Order
sections.description: In the following example, the order is updated with Bread, and the total amount recalculates to 310 SEK.

steps.title: Cancel Order

prerequisites.text: Order created and the payment is not yet completed

steps.description: Let’s say you create an order. You can cancel it for any reason, but only before the payment is completed. Once the payment is done, cancellation is no longer allowed.

sections.title: Create Order
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order is created with 2 line items

sections.title: Cancel Order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is canceled

steps.title: Cancel a Payment

prerequisites.text: Order created and payment initiated but not yet completed

steps.description: Let’s say you create an order and initiate the payment, but decide to cancel it for any reason. You can cancel the payment before it is completed, and once done transaction is fully canceled.

sections.title: Create an Order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: Next, initiate the payment for created order.

sections.title: Cancel the Payment
sections.description: You want to cancel the payment for any reason. So, you can cancel the payment using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step.

steps.title: Void a Payment

prerequisites.text: Payment completed within the same business day (before 23:00 UTC)

steps.description: Let’s say you create an order and complete the payment, but immediately find any reason to stop the payment. Now, you can quickly void the completed payment (before 23:00 UTC) to prevent incorrect payments.

sections.title: Create an Order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: Next, initiate the payment for created order.

sections.title: Void the payment
sections.description: Next, void the payment for created order.

steps.title: Refunding the purchase transaction with adjustments

prerequisites.text: Original purchase order created and paid
Adjustments (e.g., tips or surcharges) included in the original order

steps.description: Let’s say an order was created with two line items, Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, along with a tip (adjustment) of 10, bringing the total to 210. A refund is now being processed by returning full amount including adjustment (10).

sections.title: Create a order with adjustments
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order with adjustment is created.

metaData.key: This is the tip from order lines

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: In the following example, a payment is initiated for the created order.

sections.title: Create a return order with full refund
sections.description: In the following example, a return order with includeAdjustments: true to refund the tip, using the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response.

sections.title: Initiate payment for return order
sections.description: Initiate the refund against the returnorderId. So 210 will be returned as refund amount.

steps.title: Refunding the purchase transaction without adjustments

prerequisites.text: Original purchase order created and paid

steps.description: Lets say an order was created with two line items. Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, bringing the total to 200. A refund is now being processed by returning full amount.

sections.title: Create a order without adjustments
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order with adjustment is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: In the following example, a payment is initiated for the created order.

sections.title: Create a return order with full refund
sections.description: In the following example, a return order is created, using the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response.

sections.title: Initiate payment for return order
sections.description: Initiate the refund against the returnorderId . So 200 will be returned as refund amount.

steps.title: Partial refund of a purchase transaction with adjustments
steps.description: Let’s say an order was created with two line items, Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, along with a tip (adjustment) of 10, bringing the total to 210. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount, including the tip.

prerequisites.text: Original purchase order created and paid
Order includes adjustments (e.g., tips or surcharges)

sections.title: Create a order with adjustments
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order with adjustment is created.

metaData.key: This is the tip from order lines

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: In the following example, a payment is initiated for the created order.

sections.title: Partial refund of a purchase transaction with adjustments
sections.description: Let’s say an order was created with two line items, Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, along with a tip (adjustment) of 10, bringing the total to 210. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount.

tabs.label: OrderLines
tabs.title: Create return order with orderLines
tabs.description: In the following example, you create a return order with one order line (Orange juice, 100 SEK) using the original purchaseOrderId from the purchase order, and set includeAdjustments: true to refund the tip along with the partial amount..

tabs.label: TotalOrderAmount
tabs.title: Create return order with totalOrderAmount
tabs.description: In the following example, you create a return order with a total amount of 100 SEK using the original purchaseOrderId from the purchase order, and set includeAdjustments: true to refund the tip along with it.

sections.title: Initiate payment for return order
sections.description: Now initiate the refund with the return orderId (from Step 1) and the purchasePaymentId (from the initiate payment of the original purchase order) to return 110 as a partial refund

prerequisites.text: Original purchase order created and paid

steps.title: Partial refund of a purchase transaction without adjustments
steps.description: Let’s say an order was created with two line items, Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, bringing the total to 200. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount.

prerequisites.text: Original purchase order created and paid
Order includes adjustments (e.g., tips or surcharges)

sections.title: Create a order without adjustments
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order with adjustment is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: In the following example, a payment is initiated for the created order.

sections.title: Partial refund of a purchase transaction without adjustments
sections.description: Lets say an order was created with two line items. Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, bringing the total to 200. A refund is now being processed by returning full amount.

tabs.label: OrderLines
tabs.title: Create return order with orderLines
tabs.description: In the following example, a return order is created with one line item Orange juice with 100 SEK, using the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response.

tabs.label: TotalOrderAmount
tabs.title: Create return order with totalOrderAmount
tabs.description: In the following example, a return order is created with totalOrderAmount as 100 SEK to refund the partial amount, using the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response.

sections.title: Initiate payment for return order
sections.description: Now initiate the refund with the return orderId (from Step 1) and the purchasePaymentId (from the initiate payment of the original purchase order) to return 100 as a partial refund

subScenarios.title: Order with Order Line Level Calculation

steps.title: Order with Order Line Level Calculation
steps.description: Lets say an order created where the merchant sell items with campaign and shipping costs, so now in the create order orderLineLevelCalculation flag is set against the order.

tabs.label: Order Line Level Calculation set as True
tabs.title: Order with orderLineLevelCalculation set as True
tabs.description: guides/in-store-payments/quick-start/true-orderlineLevelCalculation.md

tabs.label: Order with Order Line Level Calculation set to false
tabs.title: Order with Order Line Level Calculation set to false
tabs.description: guides/in-store-payments/quick-start/false-orderlineLevelCalculation.md

steps.title: Initiate payment
steps.description: Initiate payment for order you have created in the previous step via Initiate Payment API.

steps.title: Delay Capture
steps.description: Let’s say an order is created with delayCapture: true; the actual fund capture is delayed until order fulfillment is confirmed. This is commonly used in e-commerce, digital product delivery, or service bookings, where the order is verified before processing the payment. The authorized amount will be the exact amount captured later.

sections.title: Create an order
sections.description: In the following example, order is created with delayCapture true with the total order amount of 200 SEK.

sections.title: Initiate Payment
sections.description: In the following, payment is initiated for 200 SEK.

sections.title: Capture Payment
sections.description: In the following example, the payment is captured during checkout using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step to capture the full amount.

steps.title: Pre-Authorisation
steps.description: Lets say an order is placed and pre-authorizes the card with an estimated amount. The payment initiates the estimated amount but holds off on settling the payment until the final amount is captured as mentioned in the pre-authorized amount.

prerequisites.text: Order created with controlFunction delayCapture and authorizationMode

sections.title: Create an order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created with delayCapture set to true and the authorization mode as PRE-AUTH.

sections.title: Initiate Payment
sections.description: In the following example, payment is initiated for 200 SEK.

sections.title: Capture Payment
sections.description: In the following example, the payment is captured during checkout using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step to capture the full amount.(Note: When capturing payments, you cannot capture more than 400 SEK.)

steps.title: Read Session
steps.description: When merchants receive new inventory with NFC chips, they create a read session in their POS system. During checkout, the merchant scans the NFC chip to read the product information and complete the purchase.

tabs.label: Create Read Session
tabs.title: Create Read Session
tabs.description: In the following example, NFC tags are read to identify the complete list of purchased items. To read a single product, set the mode to single.

tabs.label: Create Order with readTags
tabs.title: Create Order with readTags
tabs.description: In the following example, an order is created with controlFunctions.readTags included in the request, indicating that NFC tag reading will be used to identify products for this order.

steps.title: Partial Payment
steps.description: Lets say an order created with two line items (Orange Juice, Grape Juice) of total 200, the total order amount to be paid partially.

prerequisites.text: Order created and paid in partial payment

sections.title: Create Order with Partial Payment
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order is created with two line items.

sections.title: Initiate Payment
sections.description: Initiate payment for the previously created order by making multiple partial payments, each specifying an amount and payment method, until the total order amount is fully covered.

sections.title: Check order status
sections.description: Once you initiate the payment for an order paid in partial payments, check the status of each payment using the Fetch Order Status API.

steps.title: Refunding Partial Payments Without Adjustment
steps.description: Let’s say, an order created with two line items, orange juice and grape juice, for 200 SEK. The payment initiated in two separate payments, resulting in two payment IDs. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount to one of the payment IDs.

prerequisites.text: Order created and paid in partial payment
Initiated paymentId specified for partial refund

sections.title: Create a order without adjustments
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order with adjustment is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: In the following example, a payment is initiated for the created order.

sections.title: Refunding Partial Payments Without Adjustment
sections.description: Let’s say, an order created with two line items, orange juice and grape juice, for 200 SEK. The payment initiated in two separate payments, resulting in two payment IDs. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount to one of the payment IDs.

tabs.label: Order Line
tabs.title: Create a return order with orderLine
tabs.description: In the following, a return order is created with one line item Orange Juice (100), with the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response.

tabs.label: TotalOrderAmount
tabs.title: Create a return order with totalOrderAmount
tabs.description: In the following, a return order is created with totalOrderAmount 100, with the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response..

sections.title: Initiate payment with partial refund
sections.description: Now initiate the refund for 100 against one of the payment IDs obtained from the initiate payment response of the purchase order to the return orderId received from Step 1.

steps.title: Refunding Partial Payments With Adjustment
steps.description: Let’s say, an order created with two line items, orange juice and grape juice, along with a adjustment (tip) of 10, bringing the total to 210. The payment initiated in two separate payments, resulting in two payment IDs. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount to one of the payment IDs.

prerequisites.text: Order created with adjustments and paid in partial payment
Initiated paymentId specified for partial refund

sections.title: Create a order without adjustments
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order with adjustment is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: In the following example, a payment is initiated for the created order.

sections.title: Refunding Partial Payments With Adjustment
sections.description: Let’s say, an order created with two line items, orange juice and grape juice, along with a adjustment (tip) of 10, bringing the total to 210. The payment initiated in two separate payments, resulting in two payment IDs. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount to one of the payment IDs.

tabs.label: Order Line
tabs.title: Create a return order with orderLine
tabs.description: In the following, a return order is created for one line item Orange Juice (100), with includeAdjustments: true to also refund the 10 tip, with the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response.

tabs.label: Total Order Amount
tabs.title: Create a return order with totalOrderAmount
tabs.description: In the following, a return order is created for one line item Orange Juice (100), with includeAdjustments: true to also refund the 10 tip, with the purchaseOrderId from the original purchase order response.

sections.title: Initiate payment with partial refund
sections.description: Now initiate the refund for 100 with adjustment (tip) against one of the payment IDs obtained from the initiate payment response of the purchase order to the return orderId received from Step 1.

online.name: Online Store
online.description: Set up for e-commerce and digital payments

steps.title: Initial Setup
steps.description: Familiarize yourself with our environment for demo and live environment for production. Use appropriate API endpoints based on your integration phase.

sections.title: APIs and Environments
sections.description: For the development phase in this quick start, you'll need to generate your API credentials for the Demo environment. These credentials will be used throughout the further steps.
sections.learnMore: Learn more about APIs and Environments →

steps.title: Delay Capture
steps.description: Let’s say an order is created with delayCapture: true; the actual fund capture is delayed until order fulfillment is confirmed. This is commonly used in e-commerce, digital product delivery, or service bookings, where the order is verified before processing the payment. The authorized amount will be the exact amount captured later.

sections.description: In the following example, order is created with delayCapture true with the total order amount of 200 SEK.

steps.title: Pre-Authorisation
steps.description: Lets say an order is placed and pre-authorizes the card with an estimated amount. The payment initiates the estimated amount but holds off on settling the payment until the final amount is captured as mentioned in the pre-authorized amount.

prerequisites.text: Order created with controlFunction delayCapture and authorizationMode

sections.title: Create an order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created with pre-authorization mode. This will place a hold on the customer's card for the estimated amount (200 SEK). The final amount can be adjusted when capturing the payment.

steps.title: Initiate Payment
steps.description: In the following example, payment is initiated for 200 SEK.

sections.title: Initiate Payment
sections.description: In the following example, payment is initiated for 200 SEK.

steps.title: Capture Payment
steps.description: In the following example, the payment is captured for the final amount. This can be different from the pre-authorized amount. The system will automatically handle adjusting the authorization to match the final amount.

sections.title: Capture Payment
sections.description: In the following example, the payment is captured during checkout using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step to capture the full amount.

steps.description: Let’s say an order was created with two line items, Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, along with a tip (adjustment) of 10, bringing the total to 210. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount, including the tip.

prerequisites.text: Original purchase order created and paid
Order includes adjustments (e.g., tips or surcharges)

sections.title: Create a order with adjustments
sections.description: In the following example, a purchase order with adjustment is created.

metaData.key: This is the tip from order lines

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: In the following example, a payment is initiated for the created order.

sections.title: Partial refund of a purchase transaction with adjustments
sections.description: Let’s say an order was created with two line items, Orange Juice for 100 and Grape Juice for 100, along with a tip (adjustment) of 10, bringing the total to 210. A partial refund is now being processed by returning one part of the amount.

tabs.label: OrderLines
tabs.title: Create return order with orderLines
tabs.description: In the following example, you create a return order with one order line (Orange juice, 100 SEK) using the original purchaseOrderId from the purchase order, and set includeAdjustments: true to refund the tip along with the partial amount..

tabs.label: TotalOrderAmount
tabs.title: Create return order with totalOrderAmount
tabs.description: In the following example, you create a return order with a total amount of 100 SEK using the original purchaseOrderId from the purchase order, and set includeAdjustments: true to refund the tip along with it.

sections.title: Initiate payment for return order
sections.description: Now initiate the refund with the return orderId (from Step 1) and the purchasePaymentId (from the initiate payment of the original purchase order) to return 110 as a partial refund

steps.title: Read Session
steps.description: When merchants receive new inventory with NFC chips, they create a read session in their POS system. During checkout, the merchant scans the NFC chip to read the product information and complete the purchase.

tabs.label: Create Read Session
tabs.title: Create Read Session
tabs.description: In the following example, NFC tags are read to identify the complete list of purchased items. To read a single product, set the mode to single.

tabs.label: Create Order with readTags
tabs.title: Create Order with readTags
tabs.description: In the following example, an order is created with controlFunctions.readTags included in the request, indicating that NFC tag reading will be used to identify products for this order.

subScenarios.title: Order with Order Line Level Calculation

steps.title: Order with Order Line Level Calculation
steps.description: Lets say an order created where the merchant sell items with campaign and shipping costs, so now in the create order orderLineLevelCalculation flag is set against the order.

tabs.label: Order Line Level Calculation set as True
tabs.title: Order with orderLineLevelCalculation set as True
tabs.description: guides/in-store-payments/quick-start/true-orderlineLevelCalculation.md

tabs.label: Order with Order Line Level Calculation set to false
tabs.title: Order with Order Line Level Calculation set to false
tabs.description: guides/in-store-payments/quick-start/false-orderlineLevelCalculation.md

steps.title: Initiate payment
steps.description: Initiate payment for order you have created in the previous step via Initiate Payment API.

steps.title: Delay Capture
steps.description: Let’s say an order is created with delayCapture: true; the actual fund capture is delayed until order fulfillment is confirmed. This is commonly used in e-commerce, digital product delivery, or service bookings, where the order is verified before processing the payment. The authorized amount will be the exact amount captured later.

sections.title: Create an order
sections.description: In the following example, order is created with delayCapture true with the total order amount of 200 SEK.

sections.title: Initiate Payment
sections.description: In the following, payment is initiated for 200 SEK.

sections.title: Capture Payment
sections.description: In the following example, the payment is captured during checkout using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step to capture the full amount.

steps.title: Pre-Authorisation
steps.description: Lets say an order is placed and pre-authorizes the card with an estimated amount. The payment initiates the estimated amount but holds off on settling the payment until the final amount is captured as mentioned in the pre-authorized amount.

prerequisites.text: Order created with controlFunction delayCapture and authorizationMode

sections.title: Create an order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created with delayCapture set to true and the authorization mode as PRE-AUTH.

steps.title: Initiate Payment
steps.description: In the following example, payment is initiated for 200 SEK.

sections.title: Initiate Payment
sections.description: In the following example, payment is initiated for 200 SEK.

steps.title: Capture Payment
steps.description: In the following example, the payment is captured during checkout using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step to capture the full amount.

sections.title: Capture Payment
sections.description: In the following example, the payment is captured during checkout using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step to capture the full amount.

steps.title: Tokenization
steps.description: guides/online-payments/quick-start/tokenization.md

steps.title: Cancel a Payment

prerequisites.text: Order created and payment initiated but not yet completed

steps.description: Let’s say you create an order and initiate the payment, but decide to cancel it for any reason. You can cancel the payment before it is completed, and once done transaction is fully canceled.

sections.title: Create an Order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: Next, initiate the payment for created order.

sections.title: Cancel the Payment
sections.description: You want to cancel the payment for any reason. So, you can cancel the payment using the paymentId obtained from the initiate payment step.

steps.title: Void a in-store payment

prerequisites.text: Payment completed within the same business day (before 23:00 UTC)

steps.description: Let’s say you create an order and complete the payment, but immediately find any reason to stop the payment. Now, you can quickly void the completed payment (before 23:00 UTC) to prevent incorrect payments.

sections.title: Create an Order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created.

sections.title: Initiate payment for created order
sections.description: Next, initiate the payment for created order.

sections.title: Void the payment
sections.description: Next, void the payment for created order.

steps.title: Void a online payment

prerequisites.text: Payment completed within the same business day (before 23:00 UTC)

steps.description: Let’s say you create an order and complete the payment, but immediately find any reason to stop the payment. Now, you can quickly void the completed payment (before 23:00 UTC) to prevent incorrect payments.

sections.title: Create an Order
sections.description: In the following example, an order is created.

sections.title: Fetch Order Status
sections.description: Once you completed the order, you check it's status via Fetch Order Status API.

sections.title: Void the payment
sections.description: Next, void the payment for created order using paymentId obtained from the Fetch Order Status step.
